skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem:
Data set B consists of 10 positive integers less than 70. The list shown gives 9 of the integers from data set B.

52, 54, 53, 52, 47, 48, 49, 55, 49

The mean of these 9 integers is 51. If the mean of data set B is an integer that is greater than 51, what is the value of the largest integer from data set B?
rationale: The 9 listed integers sum to 9 · 51 = 459. Let x be the tenth integer (largest, since the others are at most 55). The mean of all 10 is (459 + x)/10. For this to be an integer greater than 51, 459 + x must be divisible by 10 and (459 + x)/10 > 51, i.e., 459 + x > 510, so x > 51. The smallest x > 51 with 459 + x divisible by 10 is x = 61 (459 + 61 = 520, mean = 52, which is an integer). Therefore the largest integer is 61.
